---
title: "Nedim Mice Brain Correlation Analysis"
output: html_notebook
---

# Packages 
```{r}
#library("BiocManager")
rm(list = ls(all.names = TRUE))
gc()
#.libPaths("/home/mbotos/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/"4.2"/")
```



```{r}
.libPaths(,c("C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Documents/R/4.4.0/"))
#unloadNamespace("limma")
```

```{r}
# BiocManager::install(c("dorothea"),force=TRUE,ask = TRUE)
# library("dorothea")
library("BiocManager")
library("dplyr")
library("tidyr")
#unloadNamespace("edgeR")
#unloadNamespace("limma")
#library("edgeR")
library("ggplot2")
library("ggrepel")
library("svglite")
library("biomaRt")
library("corrplot")
#install.packages("ggpubr")
library("ggpubr")
library("gridExtra")
```

## Read excel file
```{r}
sample_sheet = "C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Nedim/2026/data/All lungs and brain_cleaned_Dec2025_NK.xlsx"
sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain = readxl::read_excel(sample_sheet, col_names = TRUE, sheet = 4)
head(sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain)
colnames(sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain)


sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain = readxl::read_excel(sample_sheet, col_names = TRUE, sheet = 6)
head(sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain)
colnames(sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain)

sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain_coi <- sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain  |> dplyr::select(ID,`ID IVI`,`% Positive area`)
colnames(sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain_coi)
colnames(sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain)

# Need to merge colun % Positive Area from sheet 4 with Brain to plot sheet 6
sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain <- sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain |> dplyr::rename("ID"="ID...39")
sample_sheet_brain <- sample_sheet_sheet_6_brain |> dplyr::left_join(sample_sheet_sheet_4_brain_coi ,by = "ID")#c("ID...1"="ID"))

head(sample_sheet_brain$`% Positive area.y`)

#sample_sheets = sample_sheets[,-1]  # Skip first column
```

## Make groups for animals
### Early (0-4)
### Resolution (5-14)
### Clearanace (15-35)

```{r}


table(sample_sheet_brain$`ID IVI.x`,sample_sheet_brain$`Euthanasia day (dpi)`)
sample_sheet_brain$Euthanasia_day_dpi = gsub(x = sample_sheet_brain$`Euthanasia day (dpi)`,
                                             pattern ="?p\\.v$",
                                             replacement = "")
sample_sheet_brain$Euthanasia_day_dpi <- as.numeric(sample_sheet_brain$Euthanasia_day_dpi)
  

message("using latest day of euthanasia day")
multima_day <- max(sample_sheet_brain$Euthanasia_day_dpi)

sample_sheet_brain <- sample_sheet_brain  |> dplyr::mutate(
    # Time group based on euthanasia day
    Time_Group = case_when(
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 0 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= 4 ~ "Accute (0-4 dpi)",
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 5 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= 14 ~ "Resolution (5-14 dpi)",
      Euthanasia_day_dpi >= 15 & Euthanasia_day_dpi <= multima_day  ~ "Clearance (15-35 dpi)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"),
    # Vaccination status
    Vaccination_Status = factor(`Vaccinated (v), none (n)`, levels = c("n", "v"))
  )

# Create combined grouping variable
sample_sheet_brain <- sample_sheet_brain  |> dplyr::mutate(
    Combined_Group = paste(Time_Group, "-", Vaccination_Status)
  )

```

```{r}
# Select key measures for correlation analysis
output_dir <- "C:/Users/emari/OneDrive - Universitaet Bern/Nedim/2026/Results/Brains/"
Mergecorrelation_vars <- c(
  "Histopathological score lung",
  "Histopathological score brain...23",
  "qPCR swabs log10",
  "qPCR Lung (RNA/ml)",
  "qPCR Brain (RNA/ml)",
  "qPCR brain log10",
  "Live Virus log10 brain",
  "Live Virus log10 lung",
  "% Positive area.y"
)

# Function to create correlation matrix plot
create_correlation_plot <- function(data, group_name,correlation_vars=Mergecorrelation_vars) {
  # Select and clean data
  cor_data <- data |> dplyr::select(all_of(correlation_vars)) #|> #tidyr::drop_na()
  
  if(nrow(cor_data) < 2) {
    message(paste("Not enough data for group:", group_name))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Calculate correlation matrix
  cor_matrix <- cor(cor_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")#pearson
  
  # Create plot
  pdf(paste0(output_dir,"correlation_matrix_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", group_name), ".pdf"),
      width = 12, height = 10)
  
  corrplot::corrplot(cor_matrix, 
           method = "color",
           type = "lower",  # Half triangle (lower)
           tl.col = "black",
           tl.srt = 45,
           tl.cex = 0.8,
           addCoef.col = "black",
           number.cex = 0.7,
           col = colorRampPalette(c("#6D9EC1", "white", "#E46726"))(200),
           title = paste("Correlation Matrix -", group_name),
           mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0))
  
  dev.off()
  
  return(cor_matrix)
}

# Generate correlation matrices for each group
groups <- unique(sample_sheet_brain$Combined_Group)

correlation_results <- list()
for(group in groups) {
  group_data <- sample_sheet_brain |> dplyr::filter(Combined_Group == group)
  correlation_results[[group]] <- create_correlation_plot(data = group_data, group_name = group)
}

# Overall correlation (all data)
correlation_results[["Overall"]] <- create_correlation_plot(sample_sheet_brain, "Overall")

# By vaccination status only
for(vac_status in c("n", "v")) {
  vac_data <- sample_sheet_brain |> dplyr::filter(Vaccination_Status == vac_status)
  vac_label <- ifelse(vac_status == "v", "Vaccinated", "Non-vaccinated")
  correlation_results[[vac_label]] <- create_correlation_plot(vac_data, vac_label)
}

# By time group only
time_groups <- c("Early (0-5 dpi)", "Resolution (6-7 dpi)", "Clearance (8-18 dpi)")
for(time_group in time_groups) {
  time_data <- sample_sheet_brain  |> dplyr::filter(Time_Group == time_group)
  correlation_results[[time_group]] <- create_correlation_plot(time_data, time_group)
}


```


# COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS: HISTOPATHOLOGICAL SCORES VS % POSITIVE AREA
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS: HISTOPATHOLOGICAL SCORES VS % POSITIVE AREA
# ============================================================================
sample_sheet_brain$sex <- as.factor(toupper(sample_sheet_brain$sex))

# Prepare data for comparison
comparison_data <- sample_sheet_brain |> 
  dplyr::select(
    ID,
    sex,
    `Histopathological score lung`,
    `Histopathological score brain...23`,
    #`Histopathological score brain...24`,
    `% Positive area.y`,
    Time_Group,
    Vaccination_Status
  ) |> 
  drop_na(`% Positive area.y`)




# Rename for easier plotting
names(comparison_data) <- c(
  "ID",
  "sex",
  "Histopath_Lung",
  "Histopath_Brain_23",
  #"Histopath_Brain_24",
  "Algorithm_Score",
  "Time_Group",
  "Vaccination_Status"
)

# Calculate correlations for each measure
cor_lung <- cor(comparison_data$Algorithm_Score, comparison_data$Histopath_Lung, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
cor_brain_23 <- cor(comparison_data$Algorithm_Score, comparison_data$Histopath_Brain_23, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")
#cor_brain_24 <- cor(comparison_data$Algorithm_Score, comparison_data$Histopath_Brain_24, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman")

# Create individual plots
plot_lung <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Lung)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Time_Group, shape = Vaccination_Status), size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkblue", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Histopathological Score Lung vs Algorithm",
    x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
    y = "Histopathological Score Lung",
    subtitle = sprintf("Spearman r = %.3f", cor_lung)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +
  ggpubr::stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = 0.1, label.y.npc = 0.95)#pearson

plot_brain_23 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Brain_23)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Time_Group, shape = Vaccination_Status), size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Histopathological Score Brain (23) vs Algorithm",
    x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
    y = "Histopathological Score Brain (23)",
    subtitle = sprintf("Spearman r = %.3f", cor_brain_23)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = 0.1, label.y.npc = 0.95)
# 
# plot_brain_24 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Brain_24)) +
#   geom_point(aes(color = Time_Group, shape = Vaccination_Status), size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkgreen", linetype = "dashed") +
#   labs(
#     title = "Histopathological Score Brain (24) vs Algorithm",
#     x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
#     y = "Histopathological Score Brain (24)",
#     subtitle = sprintf("Spearman r = %.3f", cor_brain_24)
#   ) +
#   theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
#   theme(
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
#     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11)
#   ) +
#   stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = 0.1, label.y.npc = 0.95)

# Combine all three plots into one figure
combined_plot <- ggarrange(
  plot_lung, 
  plot_brain_23, 
  #plot_brain_24,
  ncol = 1, 
  nrow = 2,
  common.legend = TRUE,
  legend = "bottom"
)

# Add main title
combined_plot <- annotate_figure(
  combined_plot,
  top = text_grob("Algorithm Performance vs Manual Histopathological Scores", 
                  face = "bold", size = 16)
)

# Save the combined plot
ggsave(paste0(output_dir,"histopath_vs_algorithm_comparison.pdf"), 
       combined_plot, 
       width = 10, 
       height = 9, 
       dpi = 1200)

```

## STATISTICS
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# STATISTICS BY GROUP
# ============================================================================

# Calculate correlation statistics for each group
group_correlations <- comparison_data  |> 
  group_by(Time_Group, Vaccination_Status) |> 
  summarise(
    n_samples = n(),
    cor_lung = cor(Algorithm_Score, Histopath_Lung, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    cor_brain_23 = cor(Algorithm_Score, Histopath_Brain_23, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"),
    #cor_brain_24 = cor(Algorithm_Score, Histopath_Brain_24, use = "complete.obs", method="spearman"), # add any other
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Save results to CSV
write.csv(group_correlations, paste0(output_dir,"correlation_statistics_by_group.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
cat("\n=== CORRELATION SUMMARY ===\n")
cat(sprintf("\nOverall Correlations (Algorithm vs Manual Scores):\n"))
cat(sprintf("  Lung:      r = %.3f\n", cor_lung))
cat(sprintf("  Brain (23): r = %.3f\n", cor_brain_23))
#cat(sprintf("  Brain (24): r = %.3f\n", cor_brain_24))

cat("\n=== CORRELATIONS BY GROUP ===\n")
print(group_correlations)

```


```{r}
```


## VISUALIZATION by Group
```{r}
# ============================================================================
# ADDITIONAL VISUALIZATION: FACETED BY GROUP
# ============================================================================

# Create faceted plots
faceted_lung <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Lung)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status, shape=sex), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Histopathological Score Lung vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
    x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
    y = "Histopathological Score Lung"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)

ggsave("histopath_lung_faceted.pdf", faceted_lung, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)

faceted_brain_23 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Brain_23)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status, shape=sex), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black",linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Histopathological Score Brain (23) vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
    x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
    y = "Histopathological Score Brain (23)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)

ggsave(paste0("histopath_brain_23_faceted.pdf"), faceted_brain_23, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)

# faceted_brain_24 <- ggplot(comparison_data, aes(x = Algorithm_Score, y = Histopath_Brain_24)) +
#   geom_point(aes(color = Vaccination_Status), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
#   facet_wrap(~Time_Group, scales = "free") +
#   labs(
#     title = "Histopathological Score Brain (24) vs Algorithm (by Time Group)",
#     x = "% Positive Area (Algorithm)",
#     y = "Histopathological Score Brain (24)"
#   ) +
#   theme_bw() +
#   stat_cor(method = "pearson", size = 3)

# ggsave(paste0(output_dir,"histopath_brain_24_faceted.pdf", faceted_brain_24, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 1200)
```
```{r}
message("Done")
```
```{r}

```
